data.title: Remaining 4 stage-boundary validators: implementation-plan, sprint, commit, and epic-closure
data.description:
## Scope
Extend the depth-agnostic validator pattern established in Story 2 to the remaining four stage boundaries: /dso:implementation-plan, /dso:sprint, /dso:commit, and epic-closure (completion-verifier).

## Done Definitions
- /dso:implementation-plan entry/exit validator: reads upstream preplanning PRECONDITIONS; emits own at exit.
- /dso:sprint entry/exit validator: reads upstream impl-plan PRECONDITIONS; emits own at exit.
- /dso:commit entry/exit validator: reads upstream sprint PRECONDITIONS; emits own at exit.
- **Epic-closure validator** (installed in completion-verifier agent): reads upstream commit PRECONDITIONS; emits own at exit.
- All four validators inherit Story 2's depth-agnostic + schema-roundtrip self-verification + cross-stage chain verification contract.
- All four are zero-user-interaction (SC7).
- Integration test: full 5-stage chain (brainstorm → preplanning → impl-plan → sprint → commit → epic-closure) end-to-end with depth-agnostic reads.
- Per-validator unit tests parallel the Story 2 pattern.

## Dependencies
- Story 1 (helpers + baseline).
- Story 2 (validator pattern + depth-agnostic contract).
- **Story 5 (completion-verifier migration)** — the epic-closure validator and Story 5's completion-verifier consumer migration both modify the completion-verifier agent file. Without this dependency, Story 4's epic-closure writer and Story 5's legacy-format reader race within the same agent branch, producing double-write + format-mismatch hazard.

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Epic-closure validator must serialize before Story 5's compaction runs — Story 5's ordering contract pins this.
- [Maintainability] Four validator installations share the same pattern — factor shared code into a helper module to avoid drift.
